label: Total Deaths (est.)
source: US Holocaust Memorial Museum; National WWII Museum
contestNote: Estimates range from 70 to 85 million; Soviet and Chinese losses are hardest to quantify

label: Military Deaths (est.)
source: Oxford Atlas of World War II; NARA
contestNote: Includes combat, disease, and POW deaths; exact Allied and Axis figures disputed

label: Civilian Deaths (est.)
source: USHMM; National WWII Museum; Overy, R., 'The Second World War'
contestNote: Includes Holocaust, strategic bombing, starvation, and disease; civilian losses vastly exceed military

label: Holocaust Victims
source: Yad Vashem; US Holocaust Memorial Museum
contestNote: Well-documented; additional 5–6 million non-Jewish victims (Roma, disabled, POWs, etc.)

label: Duration (European Theater)
source: National WWII Museum; Imperial War Museum

label: Nations Involved
source: Oxford History of the Second World War

label: Soviet Total Deaths (est.)
source: Russian Government estimates; Krivosheev, G.F., 'Soviet Casualties and Combat Losses'
contestNote: Figures range from 20–30 million; Soviet archives remain partially restricted

label: German Total Deaths (est.)
source: German Federal Archives; Overmans, R., 'Deutsche militärische Verluste im Zweiten Weltkrieg'
contestNote: Rüdiger Overmans' 1999 study revised German military dead to 5.3 million

label: Aircraft Produced (Allied)
source: Murray, W. & Millett, A.R., 'A War To Be Won'

label: U-Boats Sunk
source: Wynn, K., 'U-boat Operations of the Second World War'; Dönitz memoirs

label: Displaced Persons (post-war)
contestNote: Includes refugees, expellees, and liberated POWs and concentration camp survivors
